---
name: frontloom-angular
description: Use this skill for Angular frontend tasks. Enforces idiomatic Angular patterns including signals, standalone components, typed reactive forms, new control flow syntax (@if, @for, @switch), dependency injection, RxJS best practices, and Angular SSR conventions. Always use alongside frontloom-core-fe for baseline quality standards.
---

# Frontloom Angular

Apply this skill to all Angular work.
Use alongside `frontloom-core-fe` for baseline frontend quality standards.

## Goal

Produce Angular code that is idiomatic, type-safe, well-structured, and production-ready.
Follow Angular's mental model — structured, dependency-injected, signal-driven.

## Principles

1. Signals are the default state primitive. Use `signal()`, `computed()`, `effect()`.
2. Standalone components are the default. `NgModule` is legacy.
3. New control flow (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) replaces structural directives in templates.
4. Dependency injection is Angular's composition primitive — use it for services, not inheritance.
5. RxJS is powerful but not always necessary — prefer signals for synchronous state, RxJS for async streams.
6. Typed reactive forms over template-driven forms for anything non-trivial.
7. `OnPush` change detection is the default for performance.

---

## Component Patterns

### Standalone Component

```ts
// user-card.component.ts
import { Component, input, output } from "@angular/core";

@Component({
  selector: "app-user-card",
  standalone: true,
  template: `
    <article class="user-card">
      <h3>{{ user().name }}</h3>
      <p>{{ user().email }}</p>
      <button (click)="select.emit(user().id)">Select</button>
    </article>
  `,
  styleUrl: "./user-card.component.css",
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class UserCardComponent {
  user = input.required<User>();
  variant = input<"compact" | "full">("full");
  select = output<string>();
}
```

### Signal Inputs

```ts
import { Component, input } from "@angular/core";

@Component({
  selector: "app-badge",
  standalone: true,
  template: `<span [class]="variant()">{{ label() }}</span>`,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class BadgeComponent {
  // Required input
  label = input.required<string>();

  // Optional input with default
  variant = input<"default" | "success" | "error">("default");
}
```

### Output Events

```ts
import { Component, output } from "@angular/core";

@Component({
  selector: "app-search",
  standalone: true,
  template: `
    <search>
      <label for="search">Search</label>
      <input id="search" (input)="onInput($event)" />
    </search>
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class SearchComponent {
  search = output<string>();

  onInput(event: Event) {
    const value = (event.target as HTMLInputElement).value;
    this.search.emit(value);
  }
}
```

### Content Projection

```ts
// card.component.ts
@Component({
  selector: "app-card",
  standalone: true,
  template: `
    <section class="card">
      <header class="card-header">
        <ng-content select="[card-header]" />
      </header>
      <div class="card-body">
        <ng-content />
      </div>
      <footer class="card-footer">
        <ng-content select="[card-footer]" />
      </footer>
    </section>
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class CardComponent {}
```

```html
<!-- Usage -->
<app-card>
  <h2 card-header>Settings</h2>
  <p>Main content goes here.</p>
  <button card-footer (click)="save()">Save</button>
</app-card>
```

---

## Signals (Reactivity)

### `signal`, `computed`, `effect`

```ts
import { signal, computed, effect } from "@angular/core";

// Writable signal
const count = signal(0);
const query = signal("");
const items = signal<Item[]>([]);

// Read: count()
// Write: count.set(5) or count.update(n => n + 1)

// Computed — derived, read-only, cached
const filteredItems = computed(() =>
  items().filter((item) => item.name.toLowerCase().includes(query().toLowerCase()))
);

const itemCount = computed(() => filteredItems().length);

// Effect — for side effects (logging, DOM sync, analytics)
effect(() => {
  console.log(`Items changed: ${items().length}`);
});
```

### Signals in Components

```ts
@Component({
  selector: "app-counter",
  standalone: true,
  template: `
    <div>
      <p>Count: {{ count() }}</p>
      <p>Double: {{ double() }}</p>
      <button (click)="increment()">+1</button>
    </div>
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class CounterComponent {
  count = signal(0);
  double = computed(() => this.count() * 2);

  increment() {
    this.count.update((n) => n + 1);
  }
}
```

### `linkedSignal` for Derived Writable State

```ts
import { signal, linkedSignal } from "@angular/core";

const selectedCategory = signal("all");

// Resets to first page when category changes, but can be set independently
const currentPage = linkedSignal(() => {
  selectedCategory(); // Track dependency
  return 1; // Reset to page 1
});

// Set independently
currentPage.set(3);

// When selectedCategory changes, currentPage resets to 1
```

### `resource` for Async Data

```ts
import { resource, signal } from "@angular/core";

const userId = signal("123");

const userResource = resource({
  request: () => ({ id: userId() }),
  loader: async ({ request, abortSignal }) => {
    const response = await fetch(`/api/users/${request.id}`, { signal: abortSignal });
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error("Failed to load user");
    return response.json() as Promise<User>;
  },
});

// In template:
// userResource.value()   — the data (or undefined)
// userResource.status()  — "idle" | "loading" | "resolved" | "error"
// userResource.error()   — the error (or undefined)
```

```html
@switch (userResource.status()) {
  @case ("loading") {
    <app-skeleton />
  }
  @case ("error") {
    <div role="alert">
      <p>{{ userResource.error()?.message }}</p>
      <button (click)="userResource.reload()">Try again</button>
    </div>
  }
  @case ("resolved") {
    <app-user-card [user]="userResource.value()!" />
  }
}
```

---

## New Control Flow

### `@if`

```html
@if (isLoading()) {
  <app-spinner />
} @else if (error()) {
  <div role="alert">{{ error() }}</div>
} @else if (items().length === 0) {
  <app-empty-state title="No items" />
} @else {
  <app-item-list [items]="items()" />
}
```

### `@for`

```html
@for (item of items(); track item.id) {
  <app-item-card [item]="item" />
} @empty {
  <p>No items found.</p>
}
```

Rules:
- Always use `track` — it's required and replaces `trackBy`.
- Track by a stable unique identifier, not index.

### `@switch`

```html
@switch (status()) {
  @case ("idle") {
    <p>Ready to start.</p>
  }
  @case ("loading") {
    <app-spinner />
  }
  @case ("success") {
    <app-results [data]="data()" />
  }
  @case ("error") {
    <app-error [message]="error()" />
  }
}
```

### `@defer` (Lazy Loading)

```html
<!-- Lazy load a component when it enters the viewport -->
@defer (on viewport) {
  <app-heavy-chart [data]="chartData()" />
} @placeholder {
  <app-chart-skeleton />
} @loading (minimum 300ms) {
  <app-spinner />
} @error {
  <p>Failed to load chart.</p>
}

<!-- Lazy load on interaction -->
@defer (on interaction) {
  <app-comment-section [postId]="postId()" />
} @placeholder {
  <button>Show comments</button>
}

<!-- Lazy load after idle -->
@defer (on idle) {
  <app-recommendations />
}
```

---

## Services and Dependency Injection

### Service Pattern

```ts
// auth.service.ts
import { Injectable, signal, computed } from "@angular/core";

@Injectable({ providedIn: "root" })
export class AuthService {
  private user = signal<User | null>(null);
  private status = signal<"idle" | "loading" | "authenticated" | "error">("idle");

  readonly currentUser = this.user.asReadonly();
  readonly isAuthenticated = computed(() => this.status() === "authenticated");
  readonly isAdmin = computed(() => this.user()?.role === "admin");

  async login(credentials: LoginCredentials): Promise<void> {
    this.status.set("loading");
    try {
      const user = await firstValueFrom(this.http.post<User>("/api/auth/login", credentials));
      this.user.set(user);
      this.status.set("authenticated");
    } catch {
      this.status.set("error");
      throw new Error("Login failed");
    }
  }

  logout(): void {
    this.user.set(null);
    this.status.set("idle");
    this.router.navigate(["/login"]);
  }

  constructor(
    private http: HttpClient,
    private router: Router,
  ) {}
}
```

### Injecting Services

```ts
@Component({
  selector: "app-dashboard",
  standalone: true,
  imports: [UserCardComponent],
  template: `
    @if (auth.isAuthenticated()) {
      <app-user-card [user]="auth.currentUser()!" />
      <button (click)="auth.logout()">Log out</button>
    }
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class DashboardComponent {
  auth = inject(AuthService);
}
```

### When to Use Services vs Signals

| Scope | Use |
|---|---|
| Local component state | `signal()` in the component |
| Shared across unrelated components | `@Injectable` service with signals |
| Derived state | `computed()` |
| Side effects (logging, analytics) | `effect()` |
| HTTP requests | `HttpClient` in a service |
| Async data with loading/error | `resource()` |

---

## Typed Reactive Forms

```ts
import { Component } from "@angular/core";
import { FormBuilder, ReactiveFormsModule, Validators } from "@angular/forms";

@Component({
  selector: "app-contact-form",
  standalone: true,
  imports: [ReactiveFormsModule],
  template: `
    <form [formGroup]="form" (ngSubmit)="onSubmit()">
      <div>
        <label for="name">Name</label>
        <input id="name" formControlName="name" />
        @if (form.controls.name.errors?.["required"] && form.controls.name.touched) {
          <p role="alert" class="error">Name is required</p>
        }
      </div>

      <div>
        <label for="email">Email</label>
        <input id="email" type="email" formControlName="email" />
        @if (form.controls.email.errors?.["email"] && form.controls.email.touched) {
          <p role="alert" class="error">Invalid email</p>
        }
      </div>

      <div>
        <label for="message">Message</label>
        <textarea id="message" formControlName="message"></textarea>
      </div>

      <button type="submit" [disabled]="form.invalid || isSubmitting()">
        {{ isSubmitting() ? "Sending…" : "Send" }}
      </button>
    </form>
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class ContactFormComponent {
  private fb = inject(FormBuilder);

  isSubmitting = signal(false);
  submitError = signal<string | null>(null);

  form = this.fb.nonNullable.group({
    name: ["", Validators.required],
    email: ["", [Validators.required, Validators.email]],
    message: ["", Validators.required],
  });

  async onSubmit() {
    if (this.form.invalid) return;

    this.isSubmitting.set(true);
    try {
      await firstValueFrom(this.contactService.send(this.form.getRawValue()));
      this.form.reset();
    } catch (err) {
      this.submitError.set("Failed to send message");
    } finally {
      this.isSubmitting.set(false);
    }
  }

  private contactService = inject(ContactService);
}
```

---

## State Management

### Decision Guide

| Scope | Solution |
|---|---|
| Local component state | `signal()` in the component |
| Derived state | `computed()` |
| Shared across components | `@Injectable` service with signals |
| Async data with loading/error | `resource()` |
| Complex async streams (debounce, retry, combine) | RxJS in a service |
| URL state (filters, pagination) | Router query params |
| Form state | Typed Reactive Forms |

### Signal-Based Service

```ts
@Injectable({ providedIn: "root" })
export class CartService {
  private items = signal<CartItem[]>([]);

  readonly cartItems = this.items.asReadonly();
  readonly totalCount = computed(() => this.items().length);
  readonly totalPrice = computed(() =>
    this.items().reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.price * item.quantity, 0)
  );

  addItem(product: Product) {
    this.items.update((items) => {
      const existing = items.find((i) => i.productId === product.id);
      if (existing) {
        return items.map((i) =>
          i.productId === product.id ? { ...i, quantity: i.quantity + 1 } : i
        );
      }
      return [...items, { productId: product.id, price: product.price, quantity: 1 }];
    });
  }

  removeItem(productId: string) {
    this.items.update((items) => items.filter((i) => i.productId !== productId));
  }
}
```

---

## RxJS — When and How

### When to Use RxJS

| Use RxJS | Use Signals |
|---|---|
| HTTP streams, WebSockets | Synchronous component state |
| Complex async orchestration (debounce, retry, combine) | Derived/computed values |
| Event streams that need operators | Simple request/response |
| Legacy code that already uses Observables | New reactive state |

### Converting Between Signals and Observables

```ts
import { toSignal, toObservable } from "@angular/core/rxjs-interop";

// Observable → Signal
const users = toSignal(this.http.get<User[]>("/api/users"), {
  initialValue: [],
});

// Signal → Observable (for piping with RxJS operators)
const query = signal("");
const query$ = toObservable(this.query);

const results$ = query$.pipe(
  debounceTime(300),
  distinctUntilChanged(),
  switchMap((q) => this.searchService.search(q)),
);
const results = toSignal(results$, { initialValue: [] });
```

### HTTP with Signals

```ts
@Injectable({ providedIn: "root" })
export class UserService {
  private http = inject(HttpClient);

  getUsers(page: number) {
    return this.http.get<ApiResponse<User[]>>("/api/users", {
      params: { page: page.toString() },
    });
  }

  createUser(data: CreateUserInput) {
    return this.http.post<User>("/api/users", data);
  }
}
```

---

## Routing

### Route Configuration

```ts
// app.routes.ts
import type { Routes } from "@angular/router";

export const routes: Routes = [
  { path: "", component: HomeComponent },
  { path: "about", component: AboutComponent },
  {
    path: "dashboard",
    loadComponent: () => import("./dashboard/dashboard.component"),
    canActivate: [authGuard],
    children: [
      { path: "", component: DashboardOverviewComponent },
      { path: "settings", loadComponent: () => import("./settings/settings.component") },
    ],
  },
  {
    path: "users/:id",
    loadComponent: () => import("./user-detail/user-detail.component"),
    resolve: { user: userResolver },
  },
  { path: "**", component: NotFoundComponent },
];
```

### Route Guards

```ts
// auth.guard.ts
import { inject } from "@angular/core";
import { Router, type CanActivateFn } from "@angular/router";

export const authGuard: CanActivateFn = () => {
  const auth = inject(AuthService);
  const router = inject(Router);

  if (auth.isAuthenticated()) return true;
  return router.createUrlTree(["/login"]);
};
```

### Resolvers

```ts
// user.resolver.ts
import { inject } from "@angular/core";
import type { ResolveFn } from "@angular/router";

export const userResolver: ResolveFn<User> = (route) => {
  const userService = inject(UserService);
  return userService.getUser(route.paramMap.get("id")!);
};
```

---

## Error Handling

### HTTP Error Interceptor

```ts
// error.interceptor.ts
import { HttpInterceptorFn, HttpErrorResponse } from "@angular/common/http";
import { inject } from "@angular/core";
import { catchError, throwError } from "rxjs";

export const errorInterceptor: HttpInterceptorFn = (req, next) => {
  const errorService = inject(ErrorService);

  return next(req).pipe(
    catchError((error: HttpErrorResponse) => {
      if (error.status === 401) {
        // Redirect to login
        inject(Router).navigate(["/login"]);
      } else if (error.status >= 500) {
        errorService.report(error);
      }
      return throwError(() => error);
    })
  );
};

// Register in app config
export const appConfig: ApplicationConfig = {
  providers: [
    provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([errorInterceptor])),
  ],
};
```

### Component Error States with `resource()`

```ts
@Component({
  selector: "app-user-profile",
  standalone: true,
  template: `
    @switch (userResource.status()) {
      @case ("loading") {
        <app-skeleton />
      }
      @case ("error") {
        <div role="alert">
          <p>Failed to load profile.</p>
          <button (click)="userResource.reload()">Try again</button>
        </div>
      }
      @case ("resolved") {
        <app-profile-card [user]="userResource.value()!" />
      }
    }
  `,
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
})
export class UserProfileComponent {
  userId = input.required<string>();

  userResource = resource({
    request: () => ({ id: this.userId() }),
    loader: async ({ request, abortSignal }) => {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/users/${request.id}`, { signal: abortSignal });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Failed to load user");
      return res.json() as Promise<User>;
    },
  });
}
```

### Global Error Handler

```ts
// error-handler.service.ts
import { ErrorHandler, Injectable } from "@angular/core";

@Injectable()
export class GlobalErrorHandler implements ErrorHandler {
  handleError(error: unknown): void {
    console.error("Unhandled error:", error);
    // Report to error tracking service
    reportToSentry(error);
  }
}

// Register in app config
export const appConfig: ApplicationConfig = {
  providers: [
    { provide: ErrorHandler, useClass: GlobalErrorHandler },
  ],
};
```

Rules:
- Use HTTP interceptors for cross-cutting error handling (401, 500, etc.).
- Use `resource()` status for component-level loading/error states.
- Register a global `ErrorHandler` for unhandled errors.
- Never swallow errors silently — always log or surface them.

---

## Performance

### `@defer` for Lazy Loading

`@defer` is Angular's built-in code splitting at the template level.

```html
<!-- Lazy load when visible -->
@defer (on viewport) {
  <app-heavy-chart [data]="chartData()" />
} @placeholder {
  <app-chart-skeleton />
} @loading (minimum 300ms) {
  <app-spinner />
} @error {
  <p>Failed to load chart.</p>
}

<!-- Lazy load on interaction -->
@defer (on interaction) {
  <app-comment-section [postId]="postId()" />
} @placeholder {
  <button>Show comments</button>
}

<!-- Lazy load when browser is idle -->
@defer (on idle) {
  <app-recommendations />
}

<!-- Prefetch when idle, render when visible -->
@defer (on viewport; prefetch on idle) {
  <app-analytics-widget />
}
```

### Route-Level Lazy Loading

```ts
// Always lazy load route components
export const routes: Routes = [
  {
    path: "dashboard",
    loadComponent: () => import("./dashboard/dashboard.component"),
  },
  {
    path: "admin",
    loadChildren: () => import("./admin/admin.routes"),
  },
];
```

### `OnPush` Change Detection

Always use `OnPush` — it skips unnecessary change detection cycles.

```ts
@Component({
  changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush,
  // ...
})
```

With signals, `OnPush` works automatically — signal reads in templates are tracked and only re-render when the signal changes.

### Bundle Optimization

```ts
// Use tree-shakeable imports
import { format } from "date-fns/format"; // Not: import { format } from "date-fns";

// Use native APIs over libraries
new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US").format(date); // Instead of moment.js

// Analyze bundle
// ng build --stats-json && npx webpack-bundle-analyzer dist/stats.json
```

Rules:
- Use `@defer` to lazy-load heavy components.
- Use `loadComponent` / `loadChildren` for route-level code splitting.
- Use `OnPush` on every component.
- Check bundle size before adding dependencies.
- Use `afterNextRender` for browser-only initialization to avoid SSR issues.

---

## SSR with Angular

```ts
// app.config.server.ts
import { ApplicationConfig } from "@angular/core";
import { provideServerRendering } from "@angular/platform-server";

export const serverConfig: ApplicationConfig = {
  providers: [provideServerRendering()],
};
```

Rules:
- Check `isPlatformBrowser` before accessing browser APIs (`window`, `document`, `localStorage`).
- Use `afterNextRender` for code that should only run in the browser after hydration.
- Use `@defer` for components that don't need SSR.

```ts
import { afterNextRender, inject, PLATFORM_ID } from "@angular/core";
import { isPlatformBrowser } from "@angular/common";

export class ChartComponent {
  private platformId = inject(PLATFORM_ID);

  constructor() {
    afterNextRender(() => {
      // Safe — only runs in the browser after hydration
      this.initChart();
    });
  }
}
```

---

## Testing Patterns

Follow the project's existing testing setup. These patterns apply to Angular with Jest/Vitest and Angular Testing Utilities.

### Component Testing

```ts
import { ComponentFixture, TestBed } from "@angular/core/testing";
import { screen } from "@testing-library/angular";
import { render } from "@testing-library/angular";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";

// Preferred: use @testing-library/angular for behavior-driven tests
test("submits the form with valid data", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();
  const onSubmit = jest.fn();

  await render(ContactFormComponent, {
    componentInputs: { onSubmit },
  });

  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Name"), "Jane");
  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Email"), "jane@example.com");
  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Send" }));

  expect(onSubmit).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
    name: "Jane",
    email: "jane@example.com",
  });
});
```

### Service Testing

```ts
import { TestBed } from "@angular/core/testing";
import { HttpTestingController, provideHttpClientTesting } from "@angular/common/http/testing";
import { provideHttpClient } from "@angular/common/http";

describe("UserService", () => {
  let service: UserService;
  let httpMock: HttpTestingController;

  beforeEach(() => {
    TestBed.configureTestingModule({
      providers: [
        UserService,
        provideHttpClient(),
        provideHttpClientTesting(),
      ],
    });

    service = TestBed.inject(UserService);
    httpMock = TestBed.inject(HttpTestingController);
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    httpMock.verify(); // Ensure no outstanding requests
  });

  test("fetches users", () => {
    const mockUsers: User[] = [{ id: "1", name: "Jane", email: "jane@test.com" }];

    service.getUsers(1).subscribe((response) => {
      expect(response.data).toEqual(mockUsers);
    });

    const req = httpMock.expectOne("/api/users?page=1");
    expect(req.request.method).toBe("GET");
    req.flush({ data: mockUsers });
  });
});
```

### Signal-Based Component Testing

```ts
test("counter increments on click", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();

  await render(CounterComponent);

  expect(screen.getByText("Count: 0")).toBeInTheDocument();

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "+1" }));

  expect(screen.getByText("Count: 1")).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

Rules:
- Prefer `@testing-library/angular` for behavior-driven tests.
- Use `HttpTestingController` for mocking HTTP in service tests.
- Test signals by asserting on rendered output, not signal values directly.
- Group tests by behavior (validation, submission, error states), not by method.

---

## Common Anti-Patterns to Avoid

### Do Not

- **Use `NgModule` in new code.** Standalone components are the standard.
- **Use structural directives (`*ngIf`, `*ngFor`).** Use built-in control flow (`@if`, `@for`).
- **Use `ngOnChanges` for derived state.** Use `computed()`.
- **Use `any` for service return types.** Type all HTTP responses.
- **Subscribe to observables without unsubscribing.** Use `toSignal`, `takeUntilDestroyed`, or `AsyncPipe`.
- **Use `Default` change detection.** Use `OnPush` on all components.
- **Put logic in templates.** Keep complex expressions in `computed()` signals.
- **Use `EventEmitter` for new code.** Use `output()` function.
- **Use `@Input()` decorator.** Use `input()` signal function.
- **Reach for RxJS when signals suffice.** Signals are simpler for synchronous state.
- **Create components without `standalone: true`.** Module-based components are legacy.
- **Use `index` as track expression in `@for`.** Track by a stable unique identifier.
- **Ignore `@defer` for heavy components.** It's free code splitting — use it.
- **Skip `OnPush` change detection.** Default change detection causes unnecessary re-renders.
- **Add dependencies without checking bundle size.** Check with bundlephobia before importing.
- **Use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` patterns.** Use Angular's built-in sanitizer or DomSanitizer with caution.
- **Forget to unsubscribe from long-lived observables.** Use `takeUntilDestroyed()` or convert to signals.

---

## When To Read References

Read only the files that match the current task:

- `references/semantic-html.md` — creating or restructuring markup
- `references/accessibility.md` — forms, dialogs, navigation, interactive UI
- `references/typescript-strict.md` — props, API data, state typing
- `references/ui-states.md` — loading, empty, error, success states
- `references/security.md` — user input, external content, links
- `references/responsive.md` — layouts, viewport-dependent UI
- `references/testing.md` — testing patterns (adapt to Angular Testing)
- `references/performance.md` — Core Web Vitals, bundle optimization
- `references/css-architecture.md` — styling decisions, theming, design tokens
- `references/review-checklist.md` — final review gate

Note: Some references contain React/Vue code examples. Translate patterns to Angular idioms — the underlying principles are the same.

## Boundaries

This skill provides Angular idioms.
For cross-framework standards (semantics, accessibility, typing, UX states), defer to `frontloom-core-fe`.
